summary: Both critical issues from round 1 are resolved: KeepServing added to both fixtures, ErrorCheck exclusion documented with valid rationale. All 4 major issues fixed. One major issue remains in TestCACertificate fallback where the event loop is never run. Overall test quality is substantially improved — 0 integration tests skipped, CONNECT used for TLS restart test, local mock server replaces httpbin.org.

suggestions.suggestion: TestCACertificate fallback cert generation (lines 143-158) creates the event loop but never runs it. The loop reference is created, addons are added, then time.sleep(1) and master.shutdown() are called synchronously. TlsConfig.running() fires only when the event loop runs — without loop.run_until_complete(), this hook never executes. Whether CertStore.from_store() is called depends on whether addons.add() triggers configure('confdir') synchronously (mitmproxy-version-dependent). The master.shutdown() call without a running loop is also a no-op or raises silently. Correct fix: run the master briefly via loop.run_until_complete(asyncio.wait_for(master.run(), timeout=3.0)) inside a try/except asyncio.TimeoutError block.
suggestions.benefit: Ensures CA cert is reliably generated in fresh Windows CI environments. Current fallback silently fails and the test then skips — acceptable today but will cause confusion if the cert ever needs to be regenerated.

suggestions.suggestion: test_connect_tunnel_established and test_tls_works_after_restart both send CONNECT to example.com:443. mitmproxy's Proxyserver must connect upstream to return 200 — if network is unavailable these tests will receive 502/timeout instead of 200. No pytest.skip() handler for network failure (unlike the old test_http_request_proxied). Tests pass in current CI because network is available, but will fail in offline environments.
suggestions.benefit: Tests survive offline CI. A local TCP listener on _free_port() as the CONNECT target would eliminate the dependency entirely.

suggestions.suggestion: _free_port() is duplicated in test_integration_proxy.py:21 and test_integration_windows.py:28. Consider extracting to conftest.py so both files share the same implementation.
suggestions.benefit: Single source of truth; avoids future divergence.

suggestions.suggestion: time.sleep(1.0) in _shutdown_proxy is an unconditional 1-second delay after loop.stop(). _wait_port_free() is already defined at line 40 and polls deterministically. Using it here would make shutdown as fast as the OS releases the port.
suggestions.benefit: Reduces total test suite wall time (every proxy-based test waits 1s unnecessarily).
